ideation: This animation sequence simulates the act of riding a mountain cable car. The robot will mimic the holding-on-to-something motion and the slight swaying that one experiences while riding a cable car. I will use the existing examples to ensure that the movements feel natural and are within the robot's range of motion. The sequence will focus on the left arm primarily, as the right arm will remain in a more relaxed position to maintain balance. Frame 1 will show the left arm slightly raised and forward, as if the robot is grasping the cable. Frame 2 will show the left arm slightly lower and more inward, mimicking a slight sway. Frame 3 will mirror Frame 1, returning to the initial grasping position. Frame 4 will show a slight upward movement of the left arm, again mimicking the swaying motion. Frame 5 will bring the left arm to a resting position. The right arm will remain mostly still, only subtly adjusting to maintain balance.

frames.beat: arms rest, ready for the ride
left arm reaches forward, gripping the cable
a gentle sway pulls the arm inward slightly
the arm dips down, settling into the car
